title: Tender Ukrainian Pork Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful one-pot dish featuring tender pork, jammy white chocolate chips, and crusty double-rise bread, perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the pork cubes in batches until browned on all sides.
Remove the browned pork from the pot and set aside, then add the chopped onion and cook until softened.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken broth, browned pork, and dried thyme to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Stir in the white chocolate chips until melted and smooth.
Add the cubed double-rise bread to the pot and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the bread is heated through.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
